source_of_truth: Markdown remains the operational source of truth. This spec is a derived machine-checkable inventory.

items.name: Feature Delivery SOP
items.description: Guide a new product feature from Human Owner intent to bounded implementation, review, QA and acceptance.
items.trigger: The Human Owner requests a new product capability, user-facing behavior or MVP feature.
stages_actions: architecture and UX clarification when needed
documentation and changelog update when required
lessons or improvement notes when useful
input_artifacts: Product vision, PRD, architecture and UX docs where applicable
output_artifacts: Documentation or changelog updates when required
Improvement notes when repeated friction appears
gates: implementation prompt approval when repository files will change
human_owner_approval_points: implementation prompt approval when repository files will change
explicit risk acceptance when review or QA leaves residual risk
items.review_qa_relationship: Feature work usually requires code review and QA. Documentation review is required when docs or onboarding guidance change. Security/privacy review is required when sensitive data, external services, auth, secrets or sandbox boundaries are affected.

items.name: Bugfix / Rework SOP
items.description: Guide defect fixes, review findings or QA findings into targeted rework without expanding scope.
items.trigger: A bug report, review finding, QA defect, failed check or Human Owner rework request is accepted for correction.
stages_actions: Human Owner approval when repository changes are needed
Human Owner acceptance or further rework
input_artifacts: Review report, QA defect, bug report or Human Owner rework request
Allowed files and forbidden actions for the rework
human_owner_approval_points: acceptance of risk when a finding is not fixed
final acceptance after re-review or QA
items.review_qa_relationship: Rework must focus on approved findings only. Re-review should check the finding and affected files. Regression checks are required when the fix touches shared behavior, lifecycle rules, user-facing behavior or previously failed areas.

items.name: AI System Evolution SOP
items.description: Guide AI_Development_System changes through controlled evolution while preserving Human Owner authority and changelog discipline.
items.trigger: The Human Owner requests AI Development System evolution, an improvement is accepted, a roadmap item becomes active or an evolution backlog item is selected.
stages_actions: Human Owner request or roadmap/backlog item
confirm scope, out of scope and allowed files
update indexes, backlog and changelog when required
run required checks or report skipped checks
review for scope control and governance consistency
input_artifacts: Evolution roadmap, backlog item, owner plan, AICP or Human Owner request
Allowed files, forbidden actions, acceptance criteria and verification mode
output_artifacts: Updated system document or bounded documentation set
Changelog entry and README version mirrors when version changes
Check results or documented verification limitations
human_owner_approval_points: approval before role, lifecycle, prompt, review, QA, security or source-of-truth changes
final acceptance of the completed evolution task
items.review_qa_relationship: AI system evolution must be reviewed for scope control, source-of-truth consistency, Human Owner approval preservation, changelog/index consistency and absence of unrelated product code changes.
